checks.query: POST https://ml-pipeline-with-ci-cd-drift-monitoring.onrender.com/predict x40 (valid varied payloads, seeded rng)

checks.note: delta may exceed burst size if other traffic hit the public endpoint

First attempt returned NaN honestly: a 5.5s one-shot burst against a freshly-woken instance lands between 15s scrapes and newly-born counter series have no in-window increase, so rate()=0. Re-measured under steady ~2 req/s for 75s (121 requests): in-process P99 9.8ms / P50 3.5ms - far below the 1.5s alert threshold, confirming the Phase 2 client-observed 696ms P99 was RTT-dominated.
baseline: 0 alerts firing before the deliberate 4xx demonstration (stage 2)

stage2_post_deploy.status: PENDING - requires the instrumentation commit to deploy via CI (user pushes)
planned_checks: churn_model_info on the LIVE endpoint shows model_version=2 (registry champion), matching /health
ChurnClientErrorSpike fired for real via a sustained burst of invalid payloads (422s) against the live /predict
Alertmanager /api/v2/alerts captured while firing; webhook receiver log captured

ChurnHigh5xxRate remains configured-but-not-fired: it counts only code=~'5..' (server faults); invalid payloads are 422s and deliberately cannot fire it, and we do not crash production to force a 5xx. Its delivery path (Prometheus->Alertmanager->webhook) is identical to the fired 4xx rule.
